steps.title: Identify the misconception needing correction
steps.content: The student must correct the false belief that Pangaea formed before Kenorland. The key signal is whether a choice directly establishes the correct chronological order, proving that Kenorland came first.

steps.title: Apply chronological evidence to the choices
steps.content: Choice B directly corrects the misunderstanding by establishing the chronological order with specific dates: Kenorland formed 2.6 billion years ago, long before Pangaea at 300 million years ago. The other choices either ignore chronology entirely or merely note a difference in timing without clarifying which came first. That matches choice B — B is correct.
